# Verify that the abstract-stamp grid is canvas-anchored and does
# NOT shift when the boundary changes. Place an AbstractDeleteVertex
# at a known (xi, yi), then enlarge the rectangle. The grid should
# extend but individual aisles should stay where they are. The
# annotation should still resolve to the same world-space vertex.

polygon outer
0,0
300,0
300,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

set stalls_per_face=4
----
set stalls_per_face=4

debug use_abstract_stamp=true
----
debug use_abstract_stamp=true

# Baseline generate on the 300x300 rectangle.
generate
----
total_stalls: 140

# Delete an interior grid vertex.
annotation delete-vertex on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-3 at=lattice:3
----
annotation delete-vertex on=grid region=0x0001000000000000 axis=x coord=-3 at=lattice:3

generate
----
total_stalls: 148

# Enlarge the rectangle on the right side. Under a canvas-anchored
# grid, the aisle at xi=-3 stays at the same world x; the
# annotation stays attached to the same world vertex. The larger
# rectangle adds more aisles on the right, so total_stalls grows.
polygon outer
0,0
400,0
400,300
0,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

generate
----
total_stalls: 162

# Shrink the rectangle back but on the OTHER side. Same logic —
# the grid doesn't follow the boundary; the annotation stays put.
polygon outer
50,0
400,0
400,300
50,300
----
polygon outer: 4 vertices

generate
----
total_stalls: 150

screenshot
----
